paper_title: Radiocarbon and Microscopic Analysis of Speleothems

methods.description: Collection and preparation of carbonate-siliceous coralloids from various locations for microscopic and radiocarbon analysis.

steps.description: Most samples examined in this study were carbonate-siliceous coralloids from various domes of the Koněprusy Caves.
steps.searchable_summary: collect carbonate-siliceous coralloids from Koněprusy Caves

steps.description: A stereomicroscope and a polarizing optical microscope were used to examine small 3D samples and standard unpolished petrographic thin sections of the coralloids.
steps.searchable_summary: examine samples using stereomicroscope and polarizing optical microscope

steps.description: For SEM analysis, six representative samples were mechanically separated, washed with distilled water, coated with gold, and examined on a Quanta 450 scanning electron microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare and examine samples using SEM

methods.description: Analysis of fluid inclusions in calcite crystals to determine petrography and salinity.

steps.description: Fluid inclusions analyses were performed on 250 µm thick, doubly polished wafers of well-crystallized calcite crystals.
steps.searchable_summary: perform fluid inclusions analysis on calcite wafers

steps.description: Microthermometric analyses were performed using the LINKAM THMSG-600 heating-freezing stage.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct microthermometric analysis using LINKAM THMSG-600

steps.description: Fluid inclusion salinities were calculated according to the method of Bodnar.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate fluid inclusion salinities using Bodnar method

methods.description: Radiocarbon dating of speleothem samples to determine the age of calcite crystals.

steps.description: Radiocarbon analysis was applied to seven speleothem samples to determine the age of the calcite crystals.
steps.searchable_summary: perform radiocarbon analysis on speleothem samples

steps.description: Sample decomposition by concentrated H3PO4, followed by purification and graphitization with pure Zn.
AMS measurements were carried using the MICADAS system and results were calibrated using OxCal 4.4 software.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct AMS measurements and calibrate results
